text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Fuentes-Delgado, Marisol
MRN: [REDACTED]
DATE OF PROCEDURE: [DATE]
DATE OF REPORT: [DATE]
SURGEON / ORDERING PROVIDER: [REDACTED], MD
PATHOLOGIST: [REDACTED], MD
SPECIMEN RECEIVED: [DATE]
SPECIMEN ID: SP-[REDACTED]-A
CLINICAL INDICATION: 58-year-old postmenopausal Hispanic woman with a palpable left breast mass at 12 o'clock position, 4 cm from nipple. Imaging (diagnostic mammogram and ultrasound) demonstrates a 3.4 x 2.9 x 3.1 cm irregular hyperdense mass, ACR BI-RADS 5. Concurrent left axillary lymphadenopathy noted on imaging. Significant family history of breast and ovarian cancer (mother with breast cancer age 42, maternal aunt with ovarian cancer). Ultrasound-guided core needle biopsy performed for tissue diagnosis.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED:
  A:  LEFT BREAST, 12 O'CLOCK POSITION, ULTRASOUND-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY
      (6 cores submitted, each 1.2-1.6 cm in length)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FINAL DIAGNOSIS:

A.  LEFT BREAST, 12 O'CLOCK POSITION, CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:
    INVASIVE DUCTAL CARCINOMA (INVASIVE CARCINOMA OF NO SPECIAL TYPE, NST),
    NOTTINGHAM HISTOLOGIC GRADE 3 (SCORE 9/9).
    See comment and ancillary studies.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A is received in formalin, labeled with the patient's name and "left breast 12 o'clock core biopsy." The container holds six tan-white to pale pink cylindrical core needle biopsy fragments, each measuring between 1.2 and 1.6 cm in length and approximately 1.5-2 mm in diameter. The cores are firm to rubbery in consistency with a homogeneous cut surface. No grossly hemorrhagic or necrotic areas are grossly appreciable, though several cores appear firm and slightly gritty on sectioning, raising suspicion for tumor. All six cores are entirely submitted in sequential cassettes (A1 through A3, two cores per cassette) and processed with standard overnight formalin fixation. Multiple levels obtained for histologic evaluation. Total tissue volume: approximately 0.9 cm3.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

Histologic sections reveal viable neoplastic tissue in five of six submitted cores. The tumor cells are arranged predominantly in solid sheets and cohesive nests with focal cord-like infiltration of the surrounding fibroadipose and fibrous stroma. A central geographic zone of comedo-type necrosis is identified within several of the solid aggregates. The stroma is desmoplastic with a moderate lymphoplasmacytic infiltrate at the periphery of the tumor nests, a finding consistent with the tumor-infiltrating lymphocyte (TIL) pattern characteristic of some triple-negative carcinomas.

Nuclear features: The tumor nuclei are markedly enlarged and vesicular with coarse chromatin and strikingly prominent, often multiple nucleoli. Nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio is high. Nuclear pleomorphism is severe (nuclear pleomorphism score 3/3). Cytoplasm is moderate in amount, eosinophilic to amphophilic, occasionally with small lipid vacuoles.

Mitotic activity: Mitotic figures are numerous and are readily identified, including atypical forms (tripolar and irregular mitoses). Mitotic count: 28 mitotic figures per 10 high-power fields (HPF; 40x objective, field diameter 0.55 mm). Mitotic score 3/3.

Gland/tubule formation: No tubular differentiation is identified; tumor cells form exclusively solid nests and sheets (tubule formation score 3/3).

Nottingham Histologic Grade: 3 + 3 + 3 = 9/9 (Grade 3, poorly differentiated).

No in-situ (ductal carcinoma in situ, DCIS) component is identified within the submitted core biopsy material. This does not exclude the presence of an in-situ component in the excision specimen.

Lymphovascular invasion: Suspicious for lymphovascular invasion; small nests of tumor cells are identified within thin-walled vascular spaces at the periphery of one core, though this finding is difficult to definitively confirm on core biopsy material and is noted as a preliminary observation.

Background breast parenchyma (where represented): No atypical lobular hyperplasia, columnar cell changes, or other high-risk lesions are identified in the limited background tissue present. Benign fibrocystic changes and occasional dilated ducts are noted.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES:

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on block A2; positive internal and external controls confirmed):

  Hormone Receptors:
  - Estrogen Receptor (ER; SP1 clone): NEGATIVE. 0% of tumor nuclei stain positively.
    Allred score: 0/8 (proportion score 0 + intensity score 0).
    Internal positive control (normal ductal epithelium): positive.
    INTERPRETATION: ER NEGATIVE (per ASCO/CAP 2020 guidelines; threshold <1% for negative).

  - Progesterone Receptor (PR; 1E2 clone): NEGATIVE. 0% of tumor nuclei stain positively.
    Allred score: 0/8.
    Internal positive control: positive.
    INTERPRETATION: PR NEGATIVE.

  HER2 (Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2):
  - HER2 by IHC (4B5 clone): 1+ (WEAK, INCOMPLETE MEMBRANE STAINING in >10% of tumor cells).
    INTERPRETATION: HER2 NEGATIVE by IHC (score 1+). HER2 ISH (FISH/SISH) not reflexively
    performed per ASCO/CAP guidelines for IHC 1+ result. [NOTE: HER2 FISH subsequently
    ordered per clinical request; see addendum below.]

  Proliferation Index:
  - Ki-67 (MIB-1 clone): POSITIVE in approximately 92% of tumor cell nuclei (high proliferative
    index). Counted per international Ki-67 in breast cancer working group guidelines (hot-spot
    and global average method; 500 cells counted).

  Basal/Myoepithelial Markers:
  - CK5/6 (D5/16 B4 clone): POSITIVE (strong, diffuse cytoplasmic staining in >70% of tumor
    cells). Supports basal-like phenotype.
  - EGFR (EGFR.25 clone): POSITIVE (2+ incomplete membrane staining, approximately 60% of
    tumor cells). EGFR expression present, consistent with basal-like TNBC subtype.

  Tumor Suppressor:
  - p53 (DO-7 clone): STRONG DIFFUSE POSITIVE (>80% of tumor nuclei, 3+ intensity).
    INTERPRETATION: Aberrant/overexpression pattern strongly suggestive of a TP53 gain-of-
    function missense mutation (rather than wild-type p53 pattern or null/complete loss pattern).
    Correlation with molecular testing recommended.

  Epithelial Markers / Differentiation:
  - E-cadherin (36B5 clone): POSITIVE (intact membranous staining in tumor cells).
    INTERPRETATION: Consistent with ductal (non-lobular) carcinoma; lobular carcinoma excluded.

  Androgen Receptor:
  - AR (AR441 clone): NEGATIVE (<1% of tumor nuclei stain positively).
    INTERPRETATION: AR NEGATIVE. LAR (luminal androgen receptor) subtype excluded.

  PD-L1 EXPRESSION (performed on block A2):
  - PD-L1 IHC 22C3 pharmDx assay (Dako/Agilent): POSITIVE.
    Combined Positive Score (CPS): 28.
    CPS calculation: [PD-L1 staining tumor cells + lymphocytes + macrophages] / total viable
    tumor cells x 100.
    INTERPRETATION: CPS >= 10 is considered positive for first-line immunotherapy eligibility
    in metastatic TNBC (per KEYNOTE-522 and KEYNOTE-355 criteria). CPS 28 indicates
    strong expression.

ADDENDUM — HER2 FLUORESCENCE IN SITU HYBRIDIZATION (FISH):
  [Result pending; ordered per clinical team request. Expected turnaround 3-5 business days.]
  [ADDENDUM ISSUED: HER2 FISH (PathVysion / Vysis dual-probe assay): NOT AMPLIFIED.
   HER2 signals per cell (mean): 2.1. CEP17 signals per cell (mean): 1.8.
   HER2/CEP17 ratio: 1.17 (< 2.0). Average HER2 copy number: 2.1 (< 4.0 signals/cell).
   INTERPRETATION: HER2 NOT AMPLIFIED. Concurrent IHC 1+ with non-amplified FISH
   confirms HER2-NEGATIVE status per ASCO/CAP 2018 guidelines (Group 5 algorithm).]

ADDENDUM — GERMLINE BRCA TESTING NOTE:
  Germline BRCA1/2 testing has been recommended given strong family history (mother with
  breast cancer age 42, maternal aunt with ovarian cancer) and triple-negative breast cancer
  diagnosis at age 58. Results from germline testing are PENDING at time of this report and
  will be issued separately through genetic counseling. Clinical correlation required.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT:

This core needle biopsy from the left breast, 12 o'clock position, demonstrates invasive carcinoma of no special type (invasive ductal carcinoma), Nottingham histologic grade 3. The morphologic features — solid growth pattern with comedo-type necrosis, markedly pleomorphic nuclei, high mitotic rate (28/10 HPF), absent tubule formation, and desmoplastic stroma — are characteristic of a high-grade triple-negative breast carcinoma. The immunophenotype confirms the triple-negative (ER-/PR-/HER2-) phenotype with a basal-like gene expression surrogate profile (CK5/6+, EGFR+, ER-, PR-, HER2-).

The aberrant p53 overexpression (strong diffuse pattern) is consistent with a TP53 hotspot gain-of-function mutation, which is encountered in approximately 70-80% of triple-negative/basal-like breast cancers. The markedly elevated Ki-67 index (92%) reflects very high proliferative activity and is characteristic of this aggressive subtype.

PD-L1 CPS of 28 by 22C3 assay is above the clinically relevant threshold (CPS >=10), which has been associated with benefit from immunotherapy (pembrolizumab) in both the neoadjuvant setting (KEYNOTE-522) and first-line metastatic setting (KEYNOTE-355).

Given the patient's strong family history and triple-negative breast cancer histology at age 58 (a presentation associated with BRCA1 germline pathogenic variants in approximately 10-20% of cases), germline genetic testing for BRCA1 and BRCA2 is strongly encouraged and has been initiated. Correlation with molecular/NGS testing on this specimen is also recommended per tumor board discussion.

No in-situ component is identified on this limited core biopsy material; assessment of the extent of DCIS and final surgical margin status must await the excision specimen. Lymphovascular invasion is suspected but cannot be definitively assessed on core biopsy.

These findings have been discussed with the treating oncology team. Tumor board conference review recommended prior to initiation of therapy.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SUMMARY OF BIOMARKER RESULTS:

  ER:         Negative (0%, Allred 0)
  PR:         Negative (0%, Allred 0)
  HER2 IHC:   Negative (1+)
  HER2 FISH:  Not amplified (HER2/CEP17 ratio 1.17) [see addendum]
  Ki-67:      92% (high)
  CK5/6:      Positive (basal-like marker)
  EGFR:       Positive (2+)
  p53:        Overexpressed (strong diffuse; likely TP53 mutation)
  E-cadherin: Positive (intact; ductal type confirmed)
  AR:         Negative (<1%)
  PD-L1 CPS:  28 (22C3 assay; POSITIVE)

  MOLECULAR SUBTYPE (surrogate IHC): TRIPLE-NEGATIVE, BASAL-LIKE

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATHOLOGIC STAGING (clinical, based on biopsy findings and imaging correlation):
  cT2 N1 M0 — Stage IIB (AJCC 8th Edition)
  [Clinical staging based on imaging; pathologic staging to be updated after definitive surgery]

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Electronically signed by:
[REDACTED], MD
Attending Pathologist, Breast Pathology
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine

Report finalized: [DATE]
Report verified: [DATE]

** END OF REPORT **
